steps.title: Spot the key contrast in how benefits occur
steps.content:
Text 1 uses the word simply when describing the benefits: “Simply by selecting Mérida, UNESCO has helped bring awareness...and provided a significant boost.” This structure treats the benefits as automatic—they flow directly from the UNESCO selection.

Text 2 introduces a sharp turn with However, then states that “cities must still create effective marketing strategies to benefit fully.” The word must and the emphasis on intentional effort reveal that the designation alone doesn't guarantee success; active marketing work is essential.

steps.title: Match the contrast to the choices
steps.content:
Text 1 frames benefits as automatic outcomes; Text 2 frames them as dependent on deliberate effort and marketing strategy.

Choice B captures exactly this split: benefits flow automatically in Text 1, but in Text 2 they require intentional work to be fully realized.

Choices A, C, and
  • D misread the texts—A invents a permanence claim neither author makes
  • C falsely suggests Text 2 questions whether benefits exist at all
  • D attributes a focus on tour guides that appears nowhere
.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
